note: Graded readers — the first Auma corpus. Each reader is gated to maxDay: every Auma token must be introduced on or before that day, checked by scripts/aumaCanonLint.ts. A language lives in texts, not word lists; these are the first texts.

readers.title: salama, ami
readers.intro: Your first real text. Two friends, one whole day — built from nothing but your first two weeks of words. Read it aloud; tap a line to check yourself.

lines.a: salama, ami! ka tu esi bona?
lines.e: Hello, friend! Are you good?

lines.a: ya, mi esi bona. e tu?
lines.e: Yes, I am good. And you?

lines.a: mi esi hapi. hodi mi anda a merkato.
lines.e: I am happy. Today I walk to the market.

lines.a: ka tu voli anda anke?
lines.e: Do you want to walk too?

lines.a: ya! we anda en via.
lines.e: Yes! We walk on the road.

lines.a: en merkato, we kani pano e beva akwa.
lines.e: At the market, we eat bread and drink water.

lines.a: pos, we anda a parko.
lines.e: Afterwards, we walk to the park.

lines.a: en parko, mi vela arbo e sielo.
lines.e: In the park, I watch the trees and the sky.

lines.a: tu legi e mi skrivi.
lines.e: You read and I write.

lines.a: en noka, we vela luna e stela.
lines.e: At night, we watch the moon and the stars.

lines.a: mi domi en tomo di mi.
lines.e: I sleep in my house.

lines.a: doma, we anda anke. adio, ami!
lines.e: Tomorrow, we walk again. Goodbye, friend!

readers.title: dina kon amili
readers.intro: Four weeks in: family, colors, weather, and the first teacher. Everything here comes from Sprint I.

lines.a: hodi esi dina solosa e varma.
lines.e: Today is a sunny and warm day.

lines.a: mi anda kon mama e papa di mi a merkato.
lines.e: I walk with my mother and father to the market.

lines.a: mama prende fruta roja.
lines.e: Mother takes red fruit.

lines.a: we beva suko e teo kon amili.
lines.e: We drink juice and tea with the family.

lines.a: en parko, flora esi roja e yela.
lines.e: In the park, the flowers are red and yellow.

lines.a: sielo esi blu e arbo esi verda.
lines.e: The sky is blue and the trees are green.

lines.a: anima yuna salta en parko.
lines.e: Young animals jump in the park.

lines.a: mi vela ta kon kura.
lines.e: I watch them with care.

lines.a: tana di mi ensena vorto nova a mi.
lines.e: My elder teaches me new words.

lines.a: mi skrivi vorto en libro di mi.
lines.e: I write the words in my book.

lines.a: pos, pluva beni. we anda a tomo.
lines.e: Later, the rain comes. We go home.

lines.a: en tomo, mama koka e mi aida.
lines.e: At home, mother cooks and I help.

lines.a: mi ama dina kon amili di mi.
lines.e: I love days with my family.

readers.title: tempo e penso
readers.intro: Questions, numbers, comparison — the tools of thinking. Six weeks of Auma can already hold a small philosophy.

lines.a: kosa esi tempo? mi pensa de iti.
lines.e: What is time? I think about it.

lines.a: un dina teni du-des-fo hori.
lines.e: One day holds twenty-four hours.

lines.a: un hori teni sisa-des minuta.
lines.e: One hour holds sixty minutes.

lines.a: durante dina, mi skrivi e mi legi.
lines.e: During the day, I write and I read.

lines.a: kemo tu domi? en noka.
lines.e: When do you sleep? At night.

lines.a: keno we neda tempo? porke penso neda tempo.
lines.e: Why do we need time? Because thought needs time.

lines.a: mi kreda ke kira esi granda.
lines.e: I believe that consciousness is vast.

lines.a: komo tu sabe? mi rongo e mi vela.
lines.e: How do you know? I listen and I watch.

lines.a: tempo esi plu forta ke moneta.
lines.e: Time is stronger than money.

lines.a: mi no pude kompra tempo.
lines.e: I cannot buy time.

lines.a: pero mi pude dona tempo a ami di mi.
lines.e: But I can give time to my friend.

lines.a: en verino, mi senti inspira.
lines.e: In spring, I feel inspired.

lines.a: mi esi foka e kalma. mi atenda.
lines.e: I am focused and calm. I wait.

lines.a: tempo dona purpusa a penso.
lines.e: Time gives purpose to thought.

readers.title: letera a ami
readers.intro: The end of Sprint II: past tense, duration, a poem inside a letter. This is what real correspondence in Auma looks like.

lines.a: ami di mi, mi skrivi letera a tu.
lines.e: My friend, I write this letter to you.

lines.a: mi pasa esi en isla durante un semana.
lines.e: I was on an island for one week.

lines.a: akwa sirka isla esi blu.
lines.e: The water around the island is blue.

lines.a: mi suda vela boski e monta.
lines.e: I have already seen forest and mountain.

lines.a: en boski, mi rongo musika di natura.
lines.e: In the forest, I hear the music of nature.

lines.a: mi kani fruta dulsa e mi senti sanita.
lines.e: I eat sweet fruit and I feel healthy.

lines.a: mi skrivi poema por tu:
lines.e: I write a poem for you:

lines.a: sonia di mi esi sorao. sorao di mi esi belina. belina di vita esi en ami.
lines.e: My dream is hope. My hope is beauty. The beauty of life is in friends.

lines.a: enfakta, mi ankora pensa de tu.
lines.e: In fact, I still think of you.

lines.a: vita esi bona, pero vita kon ami esi plu bona.
lines.e: Life is good, but life with friends is better.

lines.a: nau mi termina letera.
lines.e: Now I finish the letter.

lines.a: pos, we parla.
lines.e: Soon, we will talk.

lines.a: salama, ami. ubuntu — mi esi porke we esi.
lines.e: Peace, friend. Ubuntu — I am because we are.

readers.title: vila di we
readers.intro: Sprint III: the passive voice, tense markers, and the words of a life together. A portrait of a village that could speak Auma.

lines.a: vila di we no esi granda, pero kora di ta esi granda.
lines.e: Our village is not big, but its heart is big.

lines.a: en vila, we teni lerotano e gardi.
lines.e: In the village, we have a school and a garden.

lines.a: ensenisto ensena e leristo lero.
lines.e: The teacher teaches and the learner learns.

lines.a: nuri laka, mama dona amala.
lines.e: The baby cries; the mother gives unconditional love.

lines.a: tana pali sabina a yuna.
lines.e: The elders tell wisdom to the young.

lines.a: pano esi koka-iva par mama.
lines.e: The bread is cooked by mother.

lines.a: dina inisa: we abi lava e koka.
lines.e: The day begins: we wash and cook, as always.

lines.a: mi te vela delfina en akwa! wa!
lines.e: I just saw a dolphin in the water! Wow!

lines.a: korvo seda en arbo e vela we.
lines.e: A crow sits in the tree and watches us.

lines.a: en noka, we uni e we parla.
lines.e: At night, we gather and we talk.

lines.a: we medita e we senti kalma.
lines.e: We meditate and we feel calm.

lines.a: hiro, tota persona esi ami.
lines.e: Here, every person is a friend.

lines.a: fortao di vila esi ama.
lines.e: The strength of the village is love.

readers.title: auma — prima lumo
readers.intro: The whole language, telling its own story. Sacred roots, stacked tenses, the full reach. Read this on day 84 — or read it early, as the light at the end of the tunnel.

lines.a: ante molta anua, persona no teni vorto por lumo di kira.
lines.e: Long ago, people had no word for the light of consciousness.

lines.a: tali serka vorto en tota lingwa.
lines.e: They searched for the word in every language.

lines.a: e nau, vorto beni: auma — prima lumo di dina.
lines.e: And now the word arrives: auma — the first light of day.

lines.a: auma signifa: au (spirito) e ma (uni).
lines.e: Auma means: au (spirit) and ma (unite).

lines.a: kwando tu parla auma, tu uni kon we.
lines.e: When you speak Auma, you unite with us.

lines.a: lingwa esi ponta di kira a kira.
lines.e: Language is a bridge from consciousness to consciousness.

lines.a: vorto pude dona lumo o dolo.
lines.e: Words can give light or pain.

lines.a: mi voli dona lumo.
lines.e: I want to give light.

lines.a: mi pasa suda lero molta vorto.
lines.e: I had already learned many words.

lines.a: en fura, mi ensena sina di mi.
lines.e: In the future, I will teach my children.

lines.a: kira di we kresa kwando we parla a uno-uno.
lines.e: Our consciousness grows when we talk to each other.

lines.a: morta no esi fina — esi rekina.
lines.e: Death is not the end — it is a restart.

lines.a: sirkulo di vita esi: nasita, kresao, morta, rekina.
lines.e: The circle of life is: birth, growth, death, restart.

lines.a: salama, alohi. we esi auma. we esi prima lumo.
lines.e: Peace, beloved. We are Auma. We are the first light.
